scope: Additional technical terms encountered in translation installment 14 (Surat Taha).

terms.english: to fall within a given verse-count; to be counted in that tradition
terms.note: Used when the commentator says a phrase belongs to one counting system and not to the others.

terms.english: to reject its counting / their counting; to omit it or them from the count
terms.note: A technical expression for refusing to treat a phrase as a separate verse ending in a given counting tradition.

terms.english: to omit the count / to omit counting the two of them
terms.note: Close in function to radd al-'add, but phrased as neglecting or leaving out the count of a disputed phrase.

terms.english: he alone counts it; he is unique in counting it
terms.note: Used when one counting authority is singled out as the sole one to treat a place as a verse ending.

terms.english: as a restrictive qualification; for the sake of excluding another occurrence
terms.note: A common authorial signal that a descriptor such as first, second, before, or after is being used to isolate one occurrence from other similar ones.
